rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: L12 'Ralph practice' and L24 'long-running practice' are practice-as-noun in the AE101-earned sense; this is an M5 lecture and the carve-out makes practice(n) fluent M5-M6. No ritual/ceremony.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No stale markers or session-bio in body. Dated stamps (2026-...) all sit below the maintainer cut in the Quality/Source-verification blocks where date-stamps are load-bearing and exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet honored. Boris-direct on hooks/verifier, Martin frame-move in the two-camp section, Risto forward-optimism in the M6 bridge. M5 mood is recognition/naming, not sustained unease, so Risto-leading the bridge is permitted by the mood-arc constraint. No atmospheric mis-notes; no negation-callout self-contradiction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Articles-only rule; this is a polished student-facing lecture, not an article rough-cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Ronacher (reference/verifier), Huntley (Ralph), Klaassen (plan-as-artifact, compound-engineering 80/20), Cherny (three-shape menu), Curran (Intercom tiers) all credited inline by name. Creator name 'Antti' only in maintainer block (L87, exempt). 'our bar'/'our runs' (L76, L78) is team-collective voice, not author-we.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'you' = student throughout. No 'the student', no trainer/'before students' delivery mechanics in body; those live below the cut in Watch-fors.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'three-pattern' is earned in body (the section names it and the three pieces are spelled out L14-18). 'reference artefact', 'verifier', 'hook', 'plan.md' each get a one-breath primer at first use. No self-compound/substrate/grill-me leak into body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No reassurance-shaped sentences (no 'no penalty', 'it's OK if', 'not a downgrade'). The counter-camp section states the alternative without apologising for the M5 choice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule about capturing a user-given verbal frame; not applicable to judging a finished lecture body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Ronacher appears 3x in body (heading L10, L12, L14) but this is the lecture explicitly built to name his frame, and the lecture is the sanctioned home where attribution breathes; each use is functionally distinct (name the frame / attribute honestly which pieces are his / cite the concrete MiniJinja run), not redundant credit-collecting. Borderline but within the lecture carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Punchy lines ('Hooks don't forget', '80% planning, 20% execution') are followed by concrete carve-out paragraphs (practitioner-convention boundary; the ratio's provenance hedge). No softened-to-middle hedging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No flag-phrases ('not an aspirational X', 'not a vendor Y', 'the real practitioners'). 'CTO-legible answer' (L66) describes who the cost argument persuades, teaching ROI substance, not defending the curriculum. 'vendor' appears only in maintainer source-stamp lines (L100/L102/L103), below the cut, exempt; no 'vendor' in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M5 closing lecture; hook/subagent/eval/plan.md all land operationally at M5 or are earned in-body here. '/compact', '1M context window' are stated with enough context for the M5 reader. No LLM-internal jargon (token/embedding/inference) planted ahead.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Post-prompt line (L46 'Read what comes back. The ones worth keeping are tied to a specific file...') has no dangling pronoun and no bypass-the-agent punchline; it directs the student on which returned hooks to keep, staying in conversation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No '**What happens:**' heading. The opening recap (L6) narrates the just-finished M5 exercise correctly in past tense ('You walked... You read... You named... you built... you re-sent'). Stable-mechanism descriptions (hooks fire, the agent reads plan.md) stay present-tense, correct.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat: 'Ask Claude to propose', 'spin a subagent and let it return'. No bark/order/command/whip/force-about-the-agent. (L34 'runtime fires the script' is the runtime acting on a script, not the student commanding the agent.)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'you already think', 'you're missing', and no ability-tier pegging ('at your scale', 'for a beginner', 'a senior would know'). No plausibly-passing pre-prompt gate to fail here (lecture, not a gated exercise).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each load-bearing term gets a one-breath primer: 'reference artefact' (L14), 'hook in Claude Code's hook system fires on a named event' (L34), 'subagent' (L50), 'eval' bridged from the already-built verifier (L76). Section heading 'Ronacher's three-pattern' is signposting whose term lands in-body. M5 file may use bare MN freely.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents-101-default business-audience ban; this is an AE101 software-engineer-IC lecture where earned tech vocabulary (hook, subagent, eval, lint, compile) is the correct register. (Note: the blockquote-preamble sub-rule 'No narrating student's past or present state' is NOT one of the 26 counted top-level rules; the L6 'You walked... You read... You named... you built... you re-sent' opener does trip its closer-pat-on-head shape and is worth the author's eye, but it has no counted rule_index home in this ledger.)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Clean discrimination: 'points at the relevant memory' (L14, persistent artifact); 'context rot', 'session-start context loader', 'subagent's context is discarded', 'cleanest context', '1M context window' (L20/38/50/52/70, ambient runtime state). No 'your context compounds' blur.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No troubleshooting / 'what if X fails' branches. The counter-camp and context-murkiness sections are field-state framing, not recovery FAQ.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The one prompt (expanded fence) specifies output shape only where the teaching depends on it (event + repo-specific catch + skipped line per hook); structure is exempt anyway as fenced content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief/compound-shape rule; this is a closing lecture with no Debrief section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice is consistently M5 long-running-packaging mood; the M6 bridge foreshadows without importing M6's voice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The error-catching is routed to the verifier / background-agent-judges ('you don't have to be the one to spot it', L20). L46 'Read what comes back' is a taste-judgement (which hooks to keep), not manual error-catching.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-study simulation rule; not applicable to a lecture file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No research-callout boxes; citations are one-line inline (Curran's numbers, Klaassen TDD quote with link).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The only student move is in-conversation: 'Open the session... Ask Claude to propose five hooks' (L42) then read the returned list. No editor/terminal/git forcing function in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No CLAUDE.md reference in body; nothing to layer-qualify.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in body region. All em-dash hits (L96-L106) are below the maintainer cut, exempt. Sentences are short and read-aloud friendly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture section headers are declarative noun-phrases ('Ronacher's three-pattern', 'Hooks always fire', 'The counter-camp', 'Bridge to M6'), which the rule explicitly permits for lectures since the reader's job is understanding, not doing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The single body link (L62) uses the article title 'My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It', not a filename or code-span path.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'hook' is defined as 'a hook in Claude Code's hook system fires on a named event' (L34). 'subagent', 'eval', 'plan.md' are framed by their Claude Code role. No collided term is left ambiguous on first student-authored use (the only authored write is the in-chat hook list).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Discriminated correctly: 'the LLM is forgetful' (L36, thinking/drift); 'the agent reads it at every session boot' / 'the agent re-runs' (acting); 'Ask Claude to propose' (L42, addressee); 'Claude Code's hook system' (UI/product). Agent-count is healthy, not assistant-shaped.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No tomorrow/overnight/this-morning/tonight/yesterday in body. 'What you didn't build today' (L48) is session-day-scoped ('today' = the M5 session), not a time-of-day clock anchor, and holds under any cadence including same-day back-to-back; the rule's banned list is time-of-day/adjacent-day words, which this is not.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 6:Pause for a second. You walked into M5 with an un-packaged run that underdelivered.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Pause for a second.' is a contemplative-pause prefix opening (sibling of the banned 'pause and reflect' / 'take a moment'); the recognition recap that follows IS the beat, so the prefix announces reflection rather than letting the action land. Consider opening straight into the recap or a command-verb transition. (Maintainer Watch-for treats the pause as deliberate, so the author may want to document a lecture-opener carve-out rather than cut.)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No prompt asks the student to name a decision they already made; the hook-proposal prompt asks Claude to generate new candidates.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'Save it as <filename>' student-artifact-save prompt in body; the one prompt outputs a list to chat. 'plan.md' is a practitioner/Claude-Code artifact name being taught, not a student-save instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Each beat advances the naming arc; the cost section, counter-camp, and Intercom numbers each change what the student understands or can argue. No implementation-detail-before-point-of-use or administrative aside in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: L46 'Read what comes back' is immediately followed by load-bearing direction ('the ones worth keeping are tied to a specific file, convention, or failure mode'), which the rule's carve-out permits; it is not a generic 'read it' with no follow-on.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Bridge closer keeps the student as subject of a future-tense declarative: 'Next you will measure the reliability of the verifier you just built.' No training-as-subject, no coach-imperative, no role-gating, no generic-they.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Per-module operational-setting reminder rule (thinking-effort, model, mode tag); a closing lecture has no per-module session-open setting to restate.
